title: Pillow dependency removal closure

status_semantics.summary: Pillow is removed from both Python dependency manifests and current Python upload/import routes generate WebP thumbnails through the Go helper. This closes Pillow dependency removal without expanding live Go route ownership or removing the retained-Python normal runtime.

actual_source_evidence.fresh_packaged_runtime_evidence: scripts/smoke_go_local_artifact.ps1 invokes the built Go artifact with --thumbnail-input and --thumbnail-output and verifies a _thumb.webp WebP output.

current_owner_semantics.thumbnail_generation_owner: Go helper invoked from retained Python routes
current_owner_semantics.go_thumbnail_status: Go helper owns thumbnail encoding; Go HTTP upload routes remain local/copied-data candidates unless explicitly enabled

completion_criteria_for_A: All packaged-runtime paths that produce _thumb.webp no longer require Python/Pillow.
POST /api/upload, POST /api/upload/url, and the import helper thumbnail ownership or packaged-runtime strategy are explicitly converged.
Upload delete and cleanup behavior for _thumb.webp companion files passes the existing parity fixtures.
Pillow is removed from requirements.txt and requirements-pi.txt.
Python runtime no longer has from PIL import Image, PIL_AVAILABLE fallback wording, startup Pillow checks, or API/docs/sequence wording that implies packaged runtime still depends on Pillow.
Go unit tests, Windows build, linux/arm64 CGO_ENABLED=0 build, thumbnail parity fixtures, Go local artifact smoke, and full pytest tests/ -v pass.
A fresh packaged runtime is proven to generate _thumb.webp without installing Python/Pillow.

allowed_items: B. Runtime ownership closure for Python removal
C. Go packaged runtime release candidate
D. Live cutover and rollback proof
next_allowed_work.rule: Pillow dependency removal closure is complete. Runtime ownership closure B is now the active blocked item; do not start C/D/E until B's request-time Python blockers are Go-owned, removed, or safely externalized. Do not add decision gates, route-by-route planning gates, or new 23.8-thumb.* items.
